_comment: Stage 1.5 runtime baseline. Updated 2026-05-10 post-deploy of c1+c2+c3 (b7e5552 stderr aggregation, 80e713a Stage 3.0 column, 05ea435 stage3-watch + design doc). Two L2 count=15 smokes ran back-to-back: 019e103a (success, 15/15, chunks_failed=0 -- FIRST clean run on record) and 019e103c (partial, 12/15, scenario chunk timed out at 630s with exit 143). Per-chunk stderr aggregation WORKING -- 019e103c.stderr_tail = '--- chunk: scenario ---\n(none)\n' (timeout SIGTERMs the subprocess before stderr surfaces, hence (none); the AGGREGATION HEADER itself is the proof the c1 commit landed). G1 reachable (one clean run achieved); G2 NOT met -- score-candidate reports 16/27 (59%) on 019e103a citing 'unknown source ids' for real MITRE/NIST IDs (mitre.t1003, mitre.t1218, nist.sp800-61.containment, etc.) while handler-level citation_dropped=0. Divergence between handler input.sources[].id set and eval fixture L*-sources.json set. Root-cause is in eval/cli-typed.ts loadAttemptCandidates resolver vs eval/fixtures/L*-sources.json content -- not in the generation skills themselves.

scenario.failure: chunk failed (single failure of 5; details in attempt 019e0d59 logs)

smoke_run_prior._comment: Pre-fix snapshot kept for diff comparison.

deltas_after_2026_05_09c.scenario_partial_recovery: still 0 — single chunk fail; root cause needs log dive

rss._note: Peak with 5-type fan-out at cap=2 was 785 MiB — still 22% of the 3.5 GiB threshold.

known_gaps: PARTIAL (2026-05-10) -- Scenario + KQL + log_analysis submit_questions retry-loop. Commit 6fb4f5d corrected the scenario steps[] HARD RULE box and the kql `hint` field, but post-deploy smokes 019e1293 (scenario) and 019e128e (log_analysis) revealed two residual gaps. (1) generate-scenario/SKILL.md Quality Standards prose still said 'Individual steps use type: mcq' which the model reads before the HARD RULE box; combined with residual 'exactly once' wording the model submits MCQ-shaped steps once, gets rejected, and exits with tool_use=1 exitCode=1. (2) generate-log-analysis/SKILL.md showed log_format: 'csv' in both Output Format and HARD RULE; Zod enum is json|syslog|windows_event|freeform. Fix: SKILL.md surgical edits in commit 573aed7; both versions bumped to 2026-05-10b. Tracking re-baseline in next post-deploy smoke.

RESOLVED (2026-05-09b) — Per-type generation timeout was 60 + count*120 = 300s for count=2, exactly the boundary causing log_analysis and scenario timeouts. Fixed: bumped to 90 + count*180 = 450s for count=2. See DISALLOWED_TOOLS change in claude-code-vps.ts.

RESOLVED (2026-05-09b) — Skills were invoking Skill/ToolSearch tool_use events before submit_questions. Fixed: added Skill,ToolSearch to DISALLOWED_TOOLS; added Tool-use policy section to all generate-* SKILL.md files.
RESOLVED (2026-05-09) — Subjective fold-into-mcq removed. generate-subjective skill shipped. TYPE_SKILL_MAP includes subjective. typeEntries covers all 5 types.

RESOLVED (2026-05-09f) — Citation enforcement moved from prompt-level (advisory) to handler-level (mechanical). After Promise.allSettled, every q.knowledge_base_source_ids is validated against input.sources[].id inside filterByCitation(); questions with empty id arrays OR any invalid id are dropped and counted in citationDropped. The HARD RULE wording in the 5 SKILL.md files stays as documentation but is no longer load-bearing — a model that emits mitre.t1003 will see its question silently dropped. chunks_failed reflects only structural failures; citationDropped counts citation-rule violations separately and is written to generation_attempts.citation_dropped (migration 0043). knowledge_base_source_ids is now preserved through GeneratedQuestionDraft so the handler can see the original model-emitted IDs (not just the resolved knowledgeBaseSources objects). All three generation paths (sharded, omnibus single-call, omnibus chunked) apply the same filter.

RESOLVED (2026-05-14) — score-candidate schemaValid failures on 2026-05-09 15:40 smoke. Was a shape mismatch in loadAttemptCandidates() fixed by ce00575 (2026-05-10). Confirmed RESOLVED: score-candidate run on 3 L2 clean post-D1+D2 attempts (019e1f20, 019e1f2c, 019e1f45) on 2026-05-14 returns 153/153 pass, schemaValid=true for all 5 types.

OPEN — scenario chunk failed once (1/5). Need to inspect attempt 019e0d59 logs for the specific failure mode. Could be the same citation issue, a longer-than-expected scenario prompt hitting the 90+count*180=630s budget, or a content-policy refusal.

RESOLVED (2026-05-09e) — Forbidden field-name synonyms (stem/explanation/log_snippet/answer_key/task/correct_answer) were leaking through despite three SKILL.md revisions (2026-05-09b → c → d). Fixed at the MCP tool boundary: assessiq-mcp/submit_questions now Zod-validates content per type with .strict(); rejection returns isError=true so the model gets feedback inside its turn budget. Also changed parseToolInput in stream-json-parser.ts from first-match to last-match so a model retry after rejection captures the corrected submission.

RESOLVED (2026-05-10) — Per-chunk stderr_tail aggregation. Previously generation_attempts.stderr_tail was populated only when the WHOLE attempt failed; chunk-level failures (e.g. 2 of 5 chunks exit-1) left the column NULL. Verified on attempt 019e0deb where inspect-attempt --show-stderr returned (none). Fixed: handler (admin-generate.ts sharded path) now accumulates per-chunk stderr into chunkStderrParts with header markers ('--- chunk: <type> ---'), joins and slices the LAST 1024 bytes into aggregatedStderrTail (outer-scoped var), then passes it to tryFinalizeAttempt on both the partial-success path and (via a pre-throw call) the all-failed path. Chunk errors without stderrTail write '(none)' instead of crashing. Privacy gate unchanged — only generation-skill stderr enters the buffer. Also extended the timeout-path AppError in claude-code-vps.ts to carry stderrTail when isGenerationSkill is true.

RESOLVED (2026-05-10) — generate-rubric extended to log_analysis. SKILL.md version bumped 2026-05-08 → 2026-05-10 with a per-type input contract section and log_analysis-specific anchor-weighting rules (1 anchor per expected_finding; 2 findings → 40+30; 3+ findings → equal split with anchor_weight_total 50-75). Service (modules/04-question-bank/src/service.ts generateRubricForQuestion) now accepts log_analysis and rejects mcq + kql with UNSUPPORTED_TYPE_FOR_RUBRIC (QB_ERROR_CODES). For log_analysis, the service passes JSON.stringify(question.content) as questionText so the skill receives question + log_format + log_excerpt + expected_findings + sample_solution + hint. GenerateRubricInput.questionType extended to 'subjective' | 'scenario' | 'log_analysis' in modules/07-ai-grading/src/types.ts. Question-editor (modules/10-admin-dashboard/src/pages/question-editor.tsx) surfaces the rubric panel for log_analysis; a muted note explains the at-grade-time auto-synth fallback. bulkGenerateMissingRubrics SQL updated to include log_analysis. Admin-authored rubric overrides the at-grade-time auto-synth (existing gradeSubjective reads q.rubric column — no change needed).

CONFIRMED LIVE (2026-05-10, post-c1+c2+c3 deploy) -- Per-chunk stderr aggregation verified on attempt 019e103c: chunks_failed=1, stderr_tail = '--- chunk: scenario ---\n(none)\n'. The header marker is the canonical proof the c1 commit landed. (none) is correct for SIGTERM-killed subprocesses (timeout path produces minimal stderr before kill). Same smoke session also produced 019e103a (success, 15/15, chunks_failed=0) -- FIRST clean L2 count=15 smoke on record. G1 reachable; need 4 more clean smokes to satisfy the 5-consecutive criterion in design doc.

OPEN (2026-05-10) -- Scenario chunk timeout at 630s with exit 143 (SIGTERM). Trace shows the model emitted submit_questions THREE times (at +51s, +200s, +290s into the chunk) -- the 2nd and 3rd had tool_input_keys=[] (empty). This is a model retry loop: MCP rejects the first malformed submission, model tries empty payload, MCP rejects again, repeats until the 630s timeout fires. The same skill on attempt 019e103a (+2 min earlier) succeeded cleanly with 3 scenario questions in <630s. So the failure is non-deterministic. Root-cause options: (a) prompt-side ambiguity in generate-scenario about the submit_questions payload shape, (b) MCP rejection feedback isn't surfacing the structural error in a way the model can recover from, (c) model variance under count=3 KB-slice. Recommend: (1) read MCP submit_questions rejection messages to confirm they echo the missing/invalid keys; (2) reinforce HARD RULE language in generate-scenario SKILL.md if the rejection is structural; (3) bump scenario timeout coefficient from base+count*180 to base+count*240 (840s for count=3) as a belt-and-braces while the retry-loop is investigated.

RESOLVED (2026-05-14) — score-candidate citation divergence. The 2026-05-10 known_gap reported that score-candidate showed 16/27 (59%) valid citations on 019e103a while handler citation_dropped=0. Root-cause investigation (6d60a01) found the divergence was already fixed by cd352c7 + ce00575 (both 2026-05-10) but the known_gap was never updated. Confirmed RESOLVED: score-candidate run on 3 L2 clean post-D1+D2 attempts on 2026-05-14 returns 153/153 citationsResolve=true across all 5 types. L2-sources.json fixture is fully in sync with runtime KB. NOTE: a separate L3 citation issue exists (019e1f7d: 0/15 FAIL — wipro-soc pack has no level_fit=L3 KB sources, generates L2-tagged questions on L3 runs). This is a KB data gap, not an eval-harness bug; documented in docs/design/2026-05-10-stage-3-promotion-rollout.md § G2 measurement results. Non-blocking at Stage 3.1 (L2 pilot only).

OPEN (2026-05-14) -- L3 KB source gap on wipro-soc pack. 019e1f7d (L3 run) scores 0/15 citationsResolve=false: questions cite level_fit=L2 sources (mitre.t1059.001, mitre.t1003, etc.) not present in L3-sources.json (20 entirely different advanced-level IDs). Pack 019df000 likely has no level_fit=L3 sources, causing the handler filterKbSources to return <3 sources and fall back to the full pack KB (L2-tagged). Remediation: add ≥3 level_fit=L3 KB sources to the wipro-soc pack before Stage 3.2/3.3. Not a G2 eval bug — G2 is PASS for L2. Blocks G3 (L3 diversity runs must pass G2 before Stage 3.3→3.4). No code change needed; KB data operation only.

L2_count_15._note: Once score-candidate schema gap closes, expect candidates to score against L2-sources.json baseline at >=80% pass rate.

regression_thresholds._comment: Future smoke runs must beat or match these. Update only via a deliberate baseline refresh commit.
